title: An Enigma by Edgar Allan Poe — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
"Seldom we find," says Solomon Don Dunce,
  "Half an idea in the profoundest sonnet.
Through all the flimsy things we see at once
  As easily as through a Naples bonnet--
  Trash of all trash!--how _can_ a lady don it?
Yet heavier far than your Petrarchan stuff--
Owl-downy nonsense that the faintest puff
  Twirls into trunk-paper the while you con it."
meaning: "An Enigma" by Edgar Allan Poe is a deep poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with ""Seldom we find," says Solomon Don Dunce,   "Half an idea in the profoundest sonnet....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
